{{Short description|Kofun in Kyushu, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Kamezuka Kofun | native_name =亀塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamezukakohun 01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.234722|N|131.739583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōita, Ōita|Ōita, Ōita]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|14|5|N|131|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kamezuka Kofun'''|亀塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Sato neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōita, Ōita| Ōita]] on the island of [[Kyushu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1996.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203086|title=亀塚古墳墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref> It is one of the largest Kofuns in Ōita Prefecture, along with the Ogumayama Kofun in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|qid=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki|1=Kitsuki, Ōita}}, and is estimated to have been constructed in the {{ill|Toyo Province|en|Toyo Province|qid=Q1205043|lt=Toyokuni area|1=Toyo Province}} in the early 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Due to the recent development of residential areas, it has been found to have been part of a cluster of Kofuns, now named the "Kamezuka Kofun Cluster".
==Overview== The Kamezuka Kofun is located on a hill on the west bank of the Nyu River mouth. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is a three-tier structure with the front section facing south, with a total length of 116 meters (anterior section 52 meters wide x 7 meters high, posterior circular section 64 meters in diameter x 10 meters high). The tumulus was covered with white quartz stone as ''[[fukiishi]]'' and surrounded by cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. There are two burial facilities in the posterior circular section.The first is a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] filled with a large box-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} measuring 3.2 meters in length, and the second is a later burial located at the top of the east side. Both burial chambers have been robbed in antiquity, but fragments of armor and iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'', jasper beads, and small glass beads indicate that there were once a large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. From the construction style and remaining grave goods, the tumulus is estimated to date from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Per local legend, this tumulus is the grave of the King of Amabe (Amabe no Kimi), and there is an entry in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' indicating that a Kingdom of Amabe once existed in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Although little maintenance had been performed on the tumulus prior to its designated as a national historic site in 1996, the mound was in relatively good preservation. A restoration project has transformed the surrounding area into the "Kamezuka Kofun Park" for tourism development, and the tumulus is now decorated with replicas of ''fukiishi'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' in an effort to restore it to what is believed to have been its original appearance. The adjacent Amabe Kofun Museum has a number of diorama and displays of artifacts from ''kofun'' from various locations around the prefecture. The site is a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Sakanoichi Station|en|Sakanoichi Station|qid=Q5359526|lt=Sakanoichi Station|1=Sakanoichi Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
